content.problemStatement:
Write a program that takes three space separated numbers as input.


Prints "**Increasing**" if the numbers are in strictly increasing order,
"**Decreasing**" if they are in strictly decreasing order,
and "**Neither**" otherwise.
Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers a, b and c (1 ≤ a, b, c ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output one line containing **Increasing** if a < b < c, **Decreasing** if a > b > c, otherwise **Neither**. The asterisks are part of the required output.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. 20 < 30 < 41, so the sequence is strictly increasing.
Only one test case. 41 > 30 > 20, so the sequence is strictly decreasing.
All three numbers are equal, which is neither strictly increasing nor strictly decreasing.

solution.approach: The task is purely a comparison of three numbers. For each test case we check the two possible strict orders. If a<b and b<c we print "**Increasing**". Else if a>b and b>c we print "**Decreasing**". Any other relation (including equalities) leads to "**Neither**". This runs in O(1) per test case, so total O(T). No extra data structures are needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case
